questions.question: Reference to classical dance Mohiniyattam can be found in which of the following texts?
questions.explanation: References of Mohiniyattam can be found in the texts Vyavaharamala written in 1709 by Mazhamagalam Narayanan Namputiri and in Ghoshayatra, written later by great poet Kunjan Nambiar. This dance form of Kerala was structured into the present day classical format by the Travancore Kings, Maharaja Kartika Tirunal and his successor Maharaja Swati Tirunal. This dance form is mostly performed by women due to the delicate nature of dance form itself.

questions.question: Who among the following is a player of Esraj musical instrument?
questions.explanation: Esraj is also referred to as Dilruba instrument. Allauddin Khan was a famous Dilruba player. It is an Indian string instrument, created 300 years ago by the 10th Sikh guru, Guru Gobind Singh, based on the much older Taus. The Esraj is a modern variant of the Dilruba, differing slightly in structure.

questions.question: Bull capital of Ashokan pillar is found in which of the following place?
questions.explanation: The Rampurva bull capital is noted as one of the seven remaining animal capitals from the Pillars of Ashoka. It is composed of a lotiform base, with an abacus decorated with floral designs, and the realistic depiction of a zebu bull. The abacus in particular displays a strong influence of Greek art: it is composed of honeysuckles alternated with stylized palmettes and small rosettes. A similar kind of capital can be seen at the basis of the Sankassa elephant capital. These designs likely originated in Greek and Near-Eastern arts.

questions.question: Jhijhian is the folk dance of which of the following state?
questions.explanation: It is a folk dance of Bihar. During drought, village women pray to Lord Indra for rain. They sing and dance to please the Lord of Rain to bring rain.

questions.question: Which among the following dance is performed to celebrate birth of a child?
questions.explanation: Chhathi dance is also a ritualistic dance, performed at the birth occasion. But, this dance is performed only on the birth of a male child. Women perform this dance on the sixth day of the birth. It is a romantic dance and performed at nighttime. It is mainly performed in the state of Haryana.

questions.question: How many UNESCO world cultural heritage sites are present in India?
questions.explanation: In India, there are totally 28 cultural heritage sites. Apart from this, there are 7 natural and 1 mixed heritage site is present. They are designated as World Heritage sites by UNESCO.

questions.question: Which among the following UNESCO world Heritage site doesn't belong to Mughal architecture?
questions.explanation: Qutub Minar belongs to sultanate architecture. It includes Alai-Darwaza Gate, the masterpiece of Indo-Muslim art (built in 1311), and two mosques, including the Quwwatu'l-Islam, the oldest in northern India, built of materials reused from some 20 Brahman temples. It was built by Qutbuddin Aibak.

questions.question: Tripitakas is related to which of the following religion?
questions.explanation: They are sacred texts of Buddhism. It comprises of Suttapitaka, Vinayapitaka and Abhidhammapitaka. These texts deal with teachings of Buddha, rules of sanga etc. They were compiled in various Buddhist councils since the first council under patronage of King Ajathastru.

questions.question: Which among the following Indus Valley site is famous for its docking yard?
questions.explanation: Lothal is a IVC site in the present state of Gujarat. It is famous for the findings of Docking Yard. It was excavated besides the river Sabarmati. The literal meaning of Lothal in Guajarati language means "Mound of the dead" as Mohenjo-Daro is referred to. It is the only site to show strong evidences of prevalence of shipping industry and trade through rivers and sea route.

questions.question: "Way Dance" is a traditional dance form of which state?
questions.explanation: It is a traditional dance form of Tripura, performed by Mog Community. It is also referred to as Lamp Dance. `Way` is a name of festival that is celebrated on the day of full moon of the month in Ashvin. Lamps are dedicated to the Lord Buddha.

questions.question: Who coauthored with A.P.J Abdul Kalam the book "Wings of Fire"?
questions.explanation: Wings of fire is an autobiography of Dr. A.P.J Abdul Kalam. It was coauthored by Arun Tiwari. Arun Tiwari is a missile scientist who worked along with Dr. Kalam. He has also designed the country's first satellite-based telemedicine system.

questions.question: Which among the following book gives a dramatized version of Sonia Gandhi's life?
questions.explanation: The Red Sari, is a dramatized version of Sonia Gandhi's life. It was authored by Javier Moro. It was originally published in October 2008 in Spain in Spanish. The book tells the story of the Gandhi family through the eyes of Sonia Gandhi.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT a book written by former Presidents of India?
questions.explanation: A view from Outside is a collection of columns by P. Chidambaram, former Finance Minister. These columns were originally published in the Indian Express and Financial Express which reflected the views of the author on economic policies of the country.

questions.question: Starmus: 50 Years of Man in Space was authored by?
questions.explanation: The book is a series of talks, articles, and recollections that celebrate the human exploration of space. It is the result of the Starmus meeting in 2011, where legendary Russian and American pioneers of the space age met up for the first time to share the moments of space journey. It was edited by Brian May and David J Eicher.

questions.question: One Part Woman is an English translation of which of the following book?
questions.explanation: One-part women is an English translation of Tamil novel Mathorubhagan. It was written by Perumal Murugan. The translation was done by Aniruddhan Vasudevan and the translation has won the Sahitya Akademi's award for translation in English.

questions.question: Who among the following is the author for ModiNomics?
questions.explanation: The book was written by Sameer Kochar. The book deals with Inclusive Economics, Inclusive Governance under NDA government.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book "Flaming Tresses of Draupadi"?
questions.explanation: The book was authored by Veerapa Moily. traces the life and journey of Draupadi from her birth to her marriage with the five brothers, to losing her fortune and honour to the Kauravas in a gamble, the deep friendship she forged with Lord Krishna and finally the culmination of the great war of Kurukshetra.

questions.question: The book "The People's Maharaja" is about who among the following?
questions.explanation: The book is a biography of Capt Amarinder Singh. It was written by Khuswant Singh. It gives insight into operation Bluestar on Golden Temple and circumstances for resignation and rejoining of Captain Singh in the Congress party.

questions.question: Who among the following has co-authored the Asha Parekh's autobiography 'The Hit Girl'?
questions.explanation: The book is autobiography of the film star and it was co-authored by Khalid Mohamed. It talks about her professional life from childhood.

questions.question: The book "Final Test" is associated with which cricketer?
questions.explanation: The book talks about final test of Sachin Tendulkar. It was written by Dilip D'Souza. It gives a detailed observation of those two and a half days of final test of Sachin in Wankhede stadium.

questions.question: Who was the first woman to receive Bharat Ratna?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the first woman to receive Bharat Ratna in the year 1971. She is popularly referred to as "Iron Lady of India". She also served as Prime Minister of India. For the victory against Pakistan in the Bangladesh liberation war in 1971, she was awarded the Bharat Ratna. Bangladesh Freedom Honor was posthumously conferred on Indira Gandhi for her outstanding contributions to Bangladesh's Liberation War by Bangladesh government in 2011.

questions.question: India's first Olympic Individual medal comes from which sport?
questions.explanation: India's first individual medal in Olympics came from Wrestling in the year 1952. Prior to this India has own gold medals in team sport- Hockey. The Olympic games of that year was hosted in Helsinki, Finland.

questions.question: Pulitzer Prize was established in which year?
questions.explanation: It was established in 1917. It is presented in memory of Newspaper publisher Joseph Pulitzer. The award is administered by Columbia University in New York City. The award is presented for achievements in the field of newspaper, magazine and online journalism, literature, and musical composition in the United States. The award comprises 21 categories. The awards are presented in memory of Newspaper publisher Joseph Pulitzer.

questions.question: The first edition of International Film Festival (IFFI) was held in which among the following cities?
questions.explanation: The 1st edition of IFFI was held in Mumbai. It was organized by Government of India. Since 2004 IFFI has been an annual event and competitive. The IFFI aims at providing a common platform for the cinemas of the world to project the excellence of the film art. The IFFI's founding principles center on discovery, promotion and support of filmmaking of all genres - thus bringing together the diversity of the forms, aesthetics and contents.

questions.question: Golden Peacock Award is associated with which field?
questions.explanation: Golden Peacock Awards, instituted by the Institute of Directors (IOD), India in 1991.The purpose is to create a competition for raising overall standards and recognize the achievements of the best performing organizations. It is regarded as a benchmark of Corporate Excellence worldwide. The Awards are bestowed annually and are designed to encourage total improvement in each sector of our business.

questions.question: Darling Foundation prize is conferred by which of the following Organizations?
questions.explanation: Darling Foundation Prize is presented by World Health Organization. It is awarded for outstanding achievements in the pathology, etiology, epidemiology, therapy, prophylaxis or control of malaria. It is presented at a special ceremony during the World Health Assembly. It was created in 1929 in memory of Dr S.T. Darling.

questions.question: Who among the following Indian has won WWF's International President's Award?
questions.explanation: Rohan Chakravarty, wildlife and environment cartoonist has won WWF International President's Award 2017 for his efforts to inspire change and attitudes towards nature. The award acknowledges and encourages outstanding achievements of young people under the age of 30 who are making significant contributions to nature conservation.

questions.question: National GeoScience Awards were instituted by which among the following ministry?
options: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
questions.explanation: The National Geoscience Awards (previously known as the National Mineral Awards) were instituted by the Ministry of Mines in 1966, to honour individuals and teams of scientists for their extraordinary achievements and outstanding contributions in fundamental and applied geosciences and mining and allied fields. It includes one Award for Excellence, one Young Scientist Award and nineteen individual and / or team awards in 16 fields of geosciences. The award carries a cash prize and a certificate of citation.

questions.question: India Power Award is given for which of the following reasons?
options: To industrial units for energy efficiency
To Power generation companies for high output
To individuals contributing to power sector
To panchayats developing sustainable energy models
questions.explanation: India Power Awards is an initiative taken by the Council of Power to acknowledge and recognize the great work done by individuals and organizations in the Power sector. It was conceived in 2008. Award Categories also encompass engineering colleges, research institutes and budding engineers. Established in 1983, the Council is an umbrella organization of power utilities and acts as a neutral platform for all entities of the Energy Industry. It serves as a medium to discuss the most pertinent issues affecting the Industry, with the aim of formulating solutions and offering suggestions to appropriate authorities in the best interest of the industry.

questions.question: Packard Award is associated with which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Packard Award is now presented by IUCN's World Commission on Protected Areas to individuals or groups to recognize outstanding service to protected areas. The award includes a certificate. Cash prizes are issued in certain circumstances. It is named after Fred Packard, who served as Secretary to the Commission in the early years.
